mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a robust labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:strong labor market generally supports equities
failure_condition: If PAYEMS growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a deteriorating labor market and potential economic contraction, leading to reduced corporate profits and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5pp over 3 months, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with low and stable inflation indicate healthy consumer demand without overheating, fostering an environment conducive to corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month average growth > 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month average growth < 0.3%
evidence_refs: fact:consumer spending is a major GDP component
failure_condition: If both RSAFS growth and CPIAUCSL growth conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth during an economic expansion indicates healthy manufacturing and business investment, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:industrial output correlates with economic health
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth is positive during an expansionary regime, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals a weakening in the construction sector and broader economic demand, potentially leading to a slowdown in economic activity and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines by more than 10% month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts are a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by more than 10% MoM, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 45% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rate combined with persistent high inflation indicates aggressive monetary tightening, which increases borrowing costs and reduces corporate profitability, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases > 0.25% in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL 12-month change > 3%
failure_condition: If both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL conditions are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent positive GDP growth indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which generally supports corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:GDP growth is a primary measure of economic health
failure_condition: If GDPC1 growth is positive but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative in more than 55% of observations over any 5-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A very low unemployment rate during a late-cycle expansion often signals an overheating economy and potential wage inflation, which can lead to tighter monetary policy and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:low unemployment can precede inflation and rate hikes
failure_condition: If UNRATE is below 4.0% in a late-cycle expansion, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances, the hypothesis is rejected.
